item.dc:creator: hangeline

item.title: Toxic shit right here
item.description: I remember the lesson of tape. Anyone else? That every person you have sex with pulls tape off of you- like your soul, personhood, inner self. And the more you let them “take” you away from yourself, you end up nothing. How absolutely barbaric. And idiotic. And nonsensical. Further rant… my folks never had the talk with me because they “didn’t think they’d have to with me.” So, years later when I was assaulted, I of course didn’t go to them. And this whole idea of being dirty, or soiled, or less than (UNLESS you’re with a loving partner and that’s your jam- to each their own). Teaching my kids all the terms. Open conversation over here. Everyone wants sex (I guess unless you’re asexual, so excuse me for stepping if I am) and sex is normal and healthy and should be done safely. End rant
item.dc:creator: EmmyZannaT

item.title: Dumpety dump 28 - Things that made me blow air through my nose
item.description:
Totally.
It's a fine line.
My friend asked his kid if the car he saw was light white or dark white. Still laughing about that.
Accurate.
Those little peets
Rooney the Rabbit.
I love the idea of being off your tits on happiness.
I may have laughed way too hard at this.

item.dc:creator: sprxsie

item.title: Glad that's cleared up.
item.description:
Long personal post warning - Unfortunately, after I lost custody of all my friends in my divorce, and my brother already put up with me "swooning over girls in our teens," I have no one else to tell my story. Feel free to ignore if you don't like reading. Previously on Imgur: https://imgur.com/gallery/i7gQTjZ In short, went on date with a woman. Despite what I understood as physical interest, she hardly said anything. Confused me came to Imgur to vent, the results of which can be seen above. I sent a message to the mutual friend who set her and I up on the date and asked "Why exactly did you think that would be a good idea?" It turns out there were a couple of problems. The first Imgur spotted very quickly. To quote @RingoAteMyBaby (among a number of others): "As someone on the spectrum, she might be on the spectrum." Apparently she is indeed someone with ASD. My date had called our mutual friend to ask if the friend had told me about the ASD, because apparently I was very understanding without being told anything. Score one for accidental victories? The second problem was a language issue, in that she's not a native speaker of my language, having moved here a few years ago. As she has a name that could easily be from somewhere nearby, I made an assumption. I was told she prefers to communicate in English, but didn't know if she could "change the parameters" of our date (or something. Best translation of what she told our mutual friend.) This is not something I was able to notice on the date myself, as she spoke so little I couldn't have noticed. So I took more of Imgur's advice and asked my mutual friend what my date thought about me and received an annoyingly uninformative "You should contact her." So...
I sent a message following advice from the original thread, very directly saying "Our previous date confused me. I am interested in you. I'd like to take you out again. And as you can see, I speak English. Are you interested? One word answer is perfectly valid." And I asked for it... in ten seconds I received the response "Yes." That was it. Followed two minutes later by "Today?" Short notice. But fine. Then a third message arrived. "I struggle to process surprise. Can you tell me your plan?" Well, I knew a couple of things about her from our mutual friend at this point. She loves to read, and she's obsessed with tea. I built my date around that and suggested going to the library's quiet reading section, read some books, maybe discuss them and drink some tea. The responses I received were... enthusiastic to the say the least. Including several paragraphs about books she likes, along with shelf references so I can find them in the library. She also mentioned...
This is unfortunately something I've heard before. When our mutual friend described me, she said I was a "nice, quiet nerd who spends his time working with computers." She neglected to mention that I'm a 6'5 metalhead that looks like a viking. And not one of the hot, made for TV vikings. Think dad-bod Thor with more beard than face and tattoos everywhere but my dick. It takes people a moment to adjust to the fact I'm a gentle giant. It clearly took her three hours. Regardless, I head to the mainland and meet up with her outside her apartment. We start walking towards the library and she immediately takes my hand, which is a positive. She also doesn't say a word, but I'm prepared for that by now. Once we're at the library, she does start talking. She even asks me a few questions. But they're all utterly random. Like "how long have you been growing your beard?" and "how do you feel about men wearing nail polish?" and other non sequiturs. She also opens up about herself a bit more. Tells me that she can only read books where the language is unsophisticated. IE: She can read Dan Brown, but couldn't manage Stephen King. Which I think I can understand. Direct language is important? And if Stephen King wrote about a person eating an apple, it would be 9 pages long. And written from the viewpoint of the apple itself. And then...
She pulls out her phone, first time I've seen the thing. She sends me a text message asking if I want to eat something. When we're one meter away from each other. Literally sat at the same table. I guess she's more comfortable with text-based communication. Which is fine. We leave the library, go to eat. Not much happens there, except she becomes a little more talkative. Subjectively, at least. She's still not what someone would call chatty, but it's also very clear now that she's showing interest hapticly (haptically?) rather than vocally. But it was also the site of the rather comical conversation: Me: "Do you like music?" Her: "You'll have to be a lot more specific." Me: "Do you like live music?" Her: "That's only a little more specific." At which point I told her about an open mic night I used to frequent before Covid that re-opened. People play covers for the most part, so familiar music. She asks about the volume and, in the longest speech for her so far, explains about something called sensory overload. In the end, we figured out that it would probably be okay, and went to listen to the music. Unfortunately there were some familiar faces at the bar who pushed me into performing. I was unsure about leaving my date alone, but she actually encouraged me. So... what is there to lose, right?
This was her comment. Did I just get... autism-negged?! Honestly, this was the funniest thing that happened the whole evening. After this, the story becomes rather normal. There was a walk home, there was a kiss, etc. By the time I was back on my boat and heading home, though, she'd already sent me a text message saying that she'd enjoyed the evening and would like to see me again... accompanied by a detailed schedule of her availability for the next two weeks. So thanks, boys and girls. You got me at least one date, and looks like another on the horizon. The moral of this story: Listen to Imgur's advice. It seems we collectively know our shit.
item.dc:creator: 0xEF

item.title: Surprise, Surprise
item.description: 100% credit to Washington Post for this. I am posting it because it is paywalled and a link would not work. We knew this was going on but this essay captures it better than I’ve been able to explain. Sauce: https://www.washingtonpost.com/opinions/2022/04/27/gop-no-longer-a-party-movement-impose-christian-nationalism/
item.dc:creator: DaveSamsonite

item.title: OBGYN clinic stories
item.description: Hi. I'm Me. I want to talk about abortion. I work at an OBGYN clinic. I've seen a variety of situations and medical conditions. I would like to discuss some of them, and some notions of conception. In the view of medical coding, there are 2 types of abortions. The SAB, and the TAB. This is a wild simplification.  An SAB, or spontaneous abortions, occurs for a variety of reasons. Getting off or on a medication. Your diet is restricted. Your body is under so much stress that it can't create a new one. Glucose. The fucking moon set wrong. The fact is, this happens all the time. It's a goddamn tragedy. Sometimes it happens and we have no idea why. We call this a miscarriage. I've watched men and women weep their eyes out over this so many times. Spontaneous abortion: the miscarriage.  There is also the TAB, or therapeutic abortion. These also occur for a variety of reasons. The one that gets everyone fired up is the choice to keep body autonomy. A person doesn't want to build a child. I support this decision, but that's not the point of this. There are other reasons for this option. In the event of a multiple fertilization, the survival of any fetus sometimes requires a reduction of numbers. Sometimes a patient has cancer, and the attempt to carry will kill them both. Sometimes the fetus failed, for whatever reason, and needs to be removed. And other times, a fetus never formed. The gestational sac formed, but a body never developed.  Again, sometimes a sac forms with no embryo. No fetus. Nothing. And yet we comfort and console a patient. You didn't fail. You didn't kill a baby. There was no baby. I'm so fucking sorry. We have to induce labor for your uterus with no fetus in it. Bodies are complicated. Lives are even more complicated. Claiming life starts at conception is harmful. Every time I wand a patient for fetal tones, I worry they won't be there. The women who never grew a fetus feel like killers. The women who experienced a miscarriage feel like failures. The men who support them feel hopeless. This is a generalization from observation and my own experience (one miscarriage and three live children). I am pro-choice. I think every child should be a wanted child. I also think it's cruel to expect women to consider fertilization as humanity. Pregnancy isn't always viable, and even when it is, that doesn't mean it will come to fruition. Christians should recall that a soul enters at first breath, because even the bible understood how cruel loss could be. I'm an atheist that believes in kindness and understanding. Be kind to people. That includes women contemplating an important choice. You have no idea what anyone is fighting. We're all just people. Be kind.
item.dc:creator: mamapants

item.title: Satan Club! Oh my!
item.dc:creator: specialeyes

item.title: TLDR: Trying to interact with a manatee does more harm than good. You can look, but no touchy-touchy.
item.description:
A couple of hours ago, ViralHog released a “cute video” of some tourists giving a manatee some water. This is actually both not good for the animal, and is a federal crime. PLEASE DO NOT BOTHER THE MANATEES!! HERE’S WHY:
Here’s the video in question. Link: https://youtube.com/shorts/ftXc8XAHniM?feature=share
Manatees are especially vulnerable because they have no predators. They’re too big for other predatory animals to bother with them. Because of this, manateees don’t see things as threats like other animals do. They certainly get startled, and will move away from something they are afraid of. But they don’t have as intense of a “fight/flight” response. They see a human, they go to investigate. They don’t bite, or snarl, or show any aggression. If you give this non-aggressive WILD animal love and affection, they will associate humans with goodness, and more dangerously, will associate BOATS with goodness (because humans are on them). You can read more about that here: https://myfwc.com/education/wildlife/manatee/viewing-guidelines/
This is why it is very important that we do not bother the manatees. They are dying very quickly now. . Link: https://environmentamerica.org/blogs/environment-america-blog/ame/one-worst-years-manatees-ever
Manatees get struck by boat propellers frequently. If they survive the collision, they could still die from the wound getting infected. Manatees + boats = tragedy . Top image: https://www.savethemanatee.org/wp-content/uploads/2017/07/drs_manatee-with-multiple-boat-collision-scars_550.jpg Bottom Image: https://www.thedodo.com/boats-injure-florida-manatees-2368810374.html
The government does not fuck around when it comes to manatee protection. They don’t care if that Facebook photo is two weeks old - they will find you, and they will arrest you. Your ass will go to jail. Don’t pass go, and if you collect $200, you’ll immediately be paying that AND some in the $500+ fine. Article about four young people picture: https://cbs12.com/news/local/fwc-investigating-photo-of-people-touching-a-manatee Smithsonian article: https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smart-news/touch-a-manatee-spend-six-months-in-jail-24670630/
The best thing to do if you encounter a manatee in distress, is to call for expert help! There’s a hotline dedicated to the rescue of manatees! . https://myfwc.com/research/manatee/information/support/contact/
Manatee gonna manatee, let’s just watch them be manatees from a distance!
